meaning: vi.干预,玩弄,贿赂,损害,削弱,篡改vt.篡改n.捣棒,夯,填塞者
examples: For your safety, never tamper with these systems.
You have no right to tamper with others' affairs.
Who are they to tamper with the rules of the game?
Whatever the process, the euro zone will be tempted to tamper with the single market.
" Michael Ramsey, Lay's lawyer, weighed in, fearing "an invitation to tamper with the case.
